mechanism_narrative: A falling FEDFUNDS rate lowers the equity discount rate and supports valuation expansion, so sustained policy easing predicts positive S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when rates are already near the effective floor and further cuts do not change financial conditions.
failure_condition: Rejected if 20d SPX returns are not positive in at least 55% of periods with a 3-month decline in FEDFUNDS, or if the rolling 2-year Rank IC stays below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: A rise in UNRATE above a short-run threshold signals deteriorating household income expectations and weaker sales growth, which should pressure equities lower over the next 20 trading days. The effect should be strongest when payroll growth is also soft.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered episodes do not produce negative 20d returns in at least 60% of cases, or if the signal disappears when UNRATE rises but PAYEMS remains stable.

mechanism_narrative: Strong monthly retail sales growth in RSAFS signals resilient nominal demand and near-term revenue support for S&P 500 firms, creating positive forward returns over the next week. The effect fails when the gain is driven by inflation rather than real spending.
failure_condition: Rejected if weekly-spaced positive changes in RSAFS fail to coincide with positive 5d SPX returns above 52% of the time, or if the effect vanishes after controlling for CPIAUCSL changes.

mechanism_narrative: Rising INDPRO after a prior decline indicates cyclical output reacceleration and improving operating leverage, which should lift S&P 500 returns over the next month. The signal fails when industrial gains are accompanied by overheating inflation and rate tightening.
failure_condition: Rejected if triggered periods do not deliver positive 20d SPX returns more than 55% of the time, or if the sign reverses when CPIAUCSL inflation is accelerating.

mechanism_narrative: A steep decline in HOUST signals housing demand contraction and weaker downstream consumption, so S&P 500 returns should be negative over the next quarter. The effect is expected to be slow-moving because housing transmits to spending with a lag.
failure_condition: Rejected if 60d forward SPX returns are not negative in at least 58% of large HOUST drawdowns, or if the signal has no incremental value after the first month.

mechanism_narrative: An elevated CPIAUCSL inflation rate combined with a high FEDFUNDS policy rate tightens real financial conditions and compresses valuation multiples, producing negative S&P 500 returns over the next month. The mechanism fails when inflation is falling fast enough to offset the policy-rate burden.
failure_condition: Rejected if high-rate periods do not show negative 20d returns relative to baseline, or if the interaction term adds no predictive power beyond CPIAUCSL and FEDFUNDS separately.

mechanism_narrative: A sustained decline in PAYEMS indicates weakening labor demand and lower expected household spending, so equities should underperform over the next month. The signal is strongest when UNRATE is also rising, confirming broad labor-market deterioration.
failure_condition: Rejected if PAYEMS declines are followed by positive 20d SPX returns in more than 45% of cases, or if the joint PAYEMS-UNRATE condition does not outperform either series alone.

mechanism_narrative: When GDPC1 growth and RSAFS growth both accelerate, the economy is delivering broad final-demand momentum that should support positive S&P 500 returns over the next quarter. The signal fails when GDP strength is inventory-driven and retail sales do not confirm consumption strength.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 and RSAFS both rise sequentially
failure_condition: Rejected if joint GDPC1-RSAFS acceleration does not produce positive 60d SPX returns in at least 57% of observations, or if GDP gains without retail confirmation have no weaker effect than confirmed demand strength.
